title: Collections Framework - ArrayList and HashMap
description: Master Java Collections Framework by implementing a test result manager using ArrayList and HashMap. Collections are fundamental for managing test data, results, and configurations in automation frameworks.
instructions: Create a class `TestResultManager` with an ArrayList to store test names and a HashMap to store test results
Implement `addTestResult(String testName, String result)` to store a test and its result
Implement `getTestResult(String testName)` to retrieve a specific test result
Implement `getAllPassedTests()` to return an ArrayList of all tests with 'PASS' status
Implement `getTestCount()` to return the total number of tests
Implement `getPassRate()` to return the percentage of passed tests
learningObjectives: Understand ArrayList operations (add, get, size, iteration)
Master HashMap operations (put, get, containsKey, values)
Learn when to use List vs Map data structures

hints.content: Initialize ArrayList with new ArrayList<>() and HashMap with new HashMap<>(). Use add() for ArrayList and put(key, value) for HashMap.
To get all passed tests, iterate through the HashMap using entrySet(): for (Map.Entry<String, String> entry : testResults.entrySet()). Check if entry.getValue().equals("PASS").

Calculate pass rate using: (getAllPassedTests().size() * 100.0) / testNames.size(). Use 100.0 (double) to ensure floating-point division. Handle empty list edge case by checking if testNames.isEmpty().
